title: Provider policy candidate
description: The immutable, signable provider-enablement policy candidate (DEC-0018 packet section 9; implemented by DEC-0019). This artifact carries the COMPLETE ratified provider configuration — provider, adapter, exact model allowlist with the pinned price table, exact API endpoint and protocol version, allowed data classes, retention and training posture, every USD/token/attempt/escalation ceiling, every disabled optional surface, the live-invocation and EXP-0001 stances, the canonical secret-boundary identifiers, and the policy validity window — so the Product Owner signs exactly one exact configuration, never a summary. Every ratified value is a schema constant: a candidate carrying any other value is contract-invalid. candidate_digest is 'sha256:' + sha256 over the canonical JSON of the candidate WITHOUT the candidate_digest field (code-unit-sorted keys, no whitespace; claim-set-sha256-1.0.0 serializer); the semantic layer recomputes it. Signing target: the Product Owner signs the stored artifact's full canonical content digest (which includes candidate_digest) under the provider-enablement-approval gate; the active gateway policy must embed that same content digest. A proposed or unsigned candidate grants nothing; contract validity proves shape only.

allowed_models.description: exact ratified model IDs sorted by model_id, each with its capability tier and the pinned conservative price table (Sonnet 5 at the post-intro rate deliberately — packet section 6.1); cost reservation and settlement use exactly these prices

pricing_version.description: identifies the pinned price table; recorded in every model-run record that uses it

api_endpoint.description: the ONLY production endpoint; a policy-controlled constant, never a caller input

anthropic_api_version.description: pinned anthropic-version header value; a policy-controlled constant, never a caller input

retention_posture.description: conservative official default: automatic backend deletion within 30 days of receipt/generation with documented exceptions; NO zero-data-retention claim is made or configured

retention_statement.const: Standard Anthropic API retention accepted for synthetic data only: inputs and outputs are automatically deleted from the provider backend within 30 days, with documented exceptions (trust-and-safety flags up to 2 years, legal holds). No ZDR agreement exists; no request-state storage is used; this is a retention posture, not a zero-retention claim.
retention_statement.description: the exact human-readable retention posture the Product Owner signs (ledger C4/C5 taxonomy: request-state statelessness is never rendered as a retention claim)

training_posture.description: Anthropic Commercial Terms: API content is not used for model training by default; no opt-in is granted

live_invocation_enabled.description: live invocation is DISABLED pending a later secret-provisioning ceremony and a separately authenticated smoke-call approval; this signature does not grant it

exp_0001_execution_enabled.description: EXP-0001 execution requires its own separate later signed approval; this signature does not grant it

keychain_service.description: canonical macOS Keychain service identifier for the future credential; policy-bound so the secret boundary is not a configuration choice

keychain_account.description: canonical macOS Keychain account identifier for the future credential

valid_until.description: policy validity window end; semantic layer requires valid_until after valid_from. The active window ends at Haiku 4.5's tentative retirement floor — running past it requires a re-ratified candidate.

decision_refs.description: the ratifying enablement decision and the implementation decision, sorted

candidate_digest.description: sha256 over the canonical JSON of this candidate WITHOUT this field; recomputed by the semantic layer — a tampered candidate is contract-invalid

examples.retention_statement: Standard Anthropic API retention accepted for synthetic data only: inputs and outputs are automatically deleted from the provider backend within 30 days, with documented exceptions (trust-and-safety flags up to 2 years, legal holds). No ZDR agreement exists; no request-state storage is used; this is a retention posture, not a zero-retention claim.
